extraction.one_line_summary: LAPORTE Christophe won a chaotic flat stage sprint, beating BENNETT Sam and GROENEWEGEN Dylan after Bora-hansgrohe delayed their leadout until final 600 meters.
tactical_patterns: Bora-hansgrohe deliberately withheld leadout until 600m remaining, allowing rivals to fatigue themselves navigating technical corners first.
Jumbo-Visma positioned VINGEGAARD Jonas separately from sprint train to avoid late crashes, creating dual safety positioning system.
Multiple crashes occurred on slow-tempo flat stages where compact peloton created wheel-touching danger at 50kph through road furniture.
VAN POPPEL Danny's late leadout beat longer VERNON Ethan/SÉNÉCHAL Florian train by conserving energy through final technical sequence.

rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: positive: Won intermediate sprint for bonus seconds then finished sprint, showing dual-threat capacity beyond typical pure sprinter profile.
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: Visibly frustrated after third place, appeared to clash with BENNETT Sam in sprint suggesting positioning errors cost him.
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: Second place despite leadout disintegrating in crashes shows individual sprint power can compensate for team failures.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: Contested intermediate sprint aggressively but crashed and suffered mechanical, revealing aggressive intent but execution issues.
rider_signals.VERNON Ethan: negative: Soudal-Quick-Step invested heavily in leadout that failed to deliver result, suggesting insufficient firepower against top-tier sprinters.
rider_signals.MULLEN Ryan: positive: Executed critical penultimate leadout man role for Bora-hansgrohe's late-engaging sprint train strategy.
